LOCUST GROVE RETIREMENT VILLAGE

CCN: 395350 · MIFFLIN, PA 17058 · Juniata County

OverviewRatingsInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
69 COTTAGE ROAD, MIFFLIN, PA 17058
Phone
7174368921
Certified beds
104
Avg daily residents
67 (64% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1976-05-12
Setting
Rural
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Resident count too small for reliable rating.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-06-27 8 8 1 40 1 40
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-05-03 20 18 6 112 1 112
Total weighted health score
58.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (39)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

38 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-06-27 2025-08-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2025-06-27 2025-08-22
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-06-27 2025-08-22
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-06-27 2025-08-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-06-27 2025-08-22
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. E 2025-06-27 2025-08-22
0742 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. D 2025-06-27 2025-08-22
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2025-06-27 2025-08-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-15
0790 Provide routine and 24-hour emergency dental care for each resident. D 2024-06-18 2024-07-05
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2024-05-03 2024-03-28
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2024-05-03 2024-06-14
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. E 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0729 Verify that a nurse aide has been trained; and if they haven't worked as a nurse aide for 2 years, receive retraining. D 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-08-04 2023-08-29
